free_text: The doubly odd nucleus 144Pm (I). Energy levels
The McMaster model FN tandem Van de
Graaff accelerator
The level structure
OG
The targets (~50 mu-g/cm2) were prepared on carbon
backings (30 mu-g/cm2) by vacuum evaporation of
isotopically enriched 143Nd203 in the presence of a
thorium reducing agent.
Enge-type split-pole magnetic spectrograp.
The deuterons were detected with 50 mu-m thick Kodak
NTB nuclear emulsions with a resolution of 17 keV
full-width at half maximum.
Statistical errors are indicated.
The targets (~50 mu-g/cm2) were prepared on carbon
backings (30 mu-g/cm2) by vacuum evaporation of
isotopically enriched 143Nd203 in the presence of a
thorium reducing agent.
Enge-type split-pole magnetic spectrograp.
The tritons were detected with 50 mu-m thick Kodak NTB
nuclear emulsions with a resolution of 10 keV
full-width at half maximum.
Statistical errors are indicated.
Broad.
A thick target (~ 1 mm) of praseodymium metal (chemical
purity >99.5%).
Both small (0.9 cm3) and large (50 cm3) Ge(Li)
detectors were used. The resolution of the small
detector was 650 eV at 122 keV and that of the larger
one was 3.0 keV at 1332 keV.
No information on sources of uncertainties
On rapidly varying Compton background.
Broad.
The targets consisted of enriched 144Nd2O3 powder
mounted on a thick carbon backing with a glue prepared
from benzene and polystyrene.
Both small (0.9 cm3) and large (50 cm3) Ge(Li)
detectors were used. The resolution of the small
detector was 650 eV at 122 keV and that of the larger
one was 3.0 keV at 1332 keV.
No information on sources of uncertainties
